channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, UK
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, UK.

item.title: Sarabande Foundation: Studio Residency 2026/27 (UK-based only)
item.description:
Organisation: Sarabande Foundation

Location: London, United Kingdom. Requires a UK address and right to work in the UK; no visa sponsorship.

Award: Heavily subsidised studio space (GBP 1 per sq ft per month including utilities), mentoring from industry experts, pro bono business support, and access to the Sarabande network. Free to apply.

Duration: Residencies start November 2026.

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

The Sarabande Foundation, founded by Lee Alexander McQueen, offers subsidised London studio residencies for creatives across disciplines (fine art, fashion, design, digital), with structured mentoring and pro bono expert support. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must have a UK address and the right to work in the UK; the foundation cannot sponsor visas. Deadline 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CRASSH 25th Anniversary Exhibition: Knowledge in a Fractured World
item.description:
Organisation: CRASSH, University of Cambridge

Location: Cambridge, United Kingdom (Alison Richard Building). Open worldwide.

Award: Exhibition from 5 October to 11 December 2026; no commission taken on sales. Free to enter.

Duration: Exhibition 5 October to 11 December 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Centre for Research in the Arts, Social Sciences and Humanities (CRASSH) at the University of Cambridge marks its 25th anniversary with an open-call exhibition on the theme Knowledge in a Fractured World: how knowledge is produced, contested, and shared in a fragmented age. Accepts 2D, 3D, video, film, and sound work; a strong thematic fit for critical data and research-based practices. Open worldwide, free to enter, no commission on sales. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Modern Art Oxford: Collaborative Micro-commissions Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Modern Art Oxford

Location: Oxford, UK. Open to collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex).

Award: GBP 1,000 artist fee per selected collective, plus up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship from the Modern Art Oxford team, four months of public exhibition display, professional installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, artwork transport support within the South East, peer networking, and inclusion in a public preview event. No application fee.

Duration: Production August to November 2026; install 23 November to 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

Modern Art Oxford marks its 60th anniversary with an open call for three collaborative micro-commissions responding to the question: how can collective practice/s help us to embrace uncertainty and complexity? Collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based across the South East of England are invited to propose new small-scale experimental works for a group exhibition in the Ground Floor Gallery, running alongside a major solo exhibition by Mike Nelson, who is also on the selection panel. Proposals may take the form of multidisciplinary research, site-responsive work, grassroots organising, performance, visual art, sound, poetry or other creative practices; ideas should respond to the theme of collective practice and uncertainty, explore collaborative approaches, experiment across disciplines, be achievable within the budget and timeline, and be original to this open call (building on existing research is fine). WHO: groups of two or more people, including established artist collectives, newly formed collaborations, interdisciplinary partnerships, informal or community-led groups, and family collaborations, based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex). SUPPORT: each of the three selected collectives receives a GBP 1,000 artist fee, up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship, four months of exhibition display, installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, and transport support within the South East. TIMELINE: online briefing and Q&A 15 June 2026 (email creativelearning@modernartoxford.org.uk for the link); selection confirmed 14 August 2026; production August to November 2026; preview party 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027. Apply via Google Forms; an information pack PDF and FAQ are on the call page. DEADLINE: Sunday 26 July 2026, 23:59.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Freelands Foundation: Learning Through Making Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Freelands Foundation

Location: UK (England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, Wales); activity must occur within the United Kingdom.

Award: Up to £25,000. No application fee.

Duration: Projects of any length, taking place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. Funding provided no earlier than December 2026.

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

The Learning Through Making fund supports UK-based visual art organisations to bring opportunities for making and material exploration into their art education work. Freelands Foundation is looking for exploratory projects that embrace openness, collaboration, and iteration -- enabling organisations to work with materials in an expansive way, unpicking preconceptions about making and material literacy without predefined outcomes. ELIGIBLE ACTIVITIES: providing materials, transport, or access to expertise and facilities; action-research trials such as a new series of workshops or events developing material literacy; adding a making-focused element to existing activity. Projects may range from a few days to several months, but must take place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. AMOUNT: up to £25,000; funding provided no earlier than December 2026. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based visual art organisations run with a charitable purpose and for public benefit; eligible types include charities registered in England and Wales, Scotland, or Northern Ireland; exempt charities; galleries, museums, or organisations within a Local Authority or larger charitable body (e.g. universities); and Community Interest Companies or Community Benefit Societies meeting specific criteria (charitable purposes, asset lock, no dividend distribution, ability to segregate restricted funds). Organisations with a prior Freelands Foundation grant relationship are eligible; you may not apply for activity already funded by Freelands Foundation. Individuals are not eligible. HOW TO APPLY: download the Application Form, Application Guidance, and optional Access Form from freelandsfoundation.co.uk; email completed form to grants@freelandsfoundation.co.uk with subject line 'LTM Fund - Application'; one additional page of images or diagrams may be included. APPLICATION SUPPORT SESSIONS (online): 9 July 2026, 10:30am-12pm; 12 August 2026, 10:30am-12pm. KEY DATES: applications open 17 June 2026; close 11 September 2026 at 12pm; outcomes shared 23 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Features Production Fund (UK, Documentary)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK

Award: Up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Feature-length non-fiction films for theatrical release; rolling two-stage process (EOI then full application)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The BFI Doc Society Features Production Fund (delivered via BFI with Doc Society as delegate partner) supports UK filmmakers producing non-fiction feature films intended for theatrical release. Two strands: Directions (first- or second-time directors) and Momentum (established talent). AMOUNT: up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant (the total Doc Society contribution per project cannot exceed £150,000). ELIGIBILITY: UK-based filmmakers, both emerging and established directors; experienced and debut producers eligible; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. PROCESS: two-stage — submit an Expression of Interest, then a Full Application if invited. Rolling intake year-round. Opened May 2026. APPLY via BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Made of Truth – Short Non-Fiction Film Fund (UK)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK (both director and producer must be UK residents)

Award: Up to £25,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Short films 5–40 minutes; up to 13 projects funded per year

Deadline: Closed 01 Jul 2026

BFI Doc Society's Made of Truth fund supports early-career UK filmmakers making non-fiction live-action or animated short films of 5–40 minutes. Up to 13 projects are funded per annual round. AMOUNT: up to £25,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: early-career directors and producers who are UK residents; teams must have at least two people (a producer and a director); directors cannot have received a commercial distribution deal for a non-fiction feature in the last five years; a reasonable proportion of early-career crew is required. Applications must meet at least three of six priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. OPENS: 18 May 2026 at 1pm UK time. DEADLINE: 1 July 2026 at 1pm UK time. APPLY via the BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Expanded Screen Fund (UK, VR/AR/Immersive Non-Fiction)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK (projects must demonstrate UK-wide benefit)

Award: £25,000–£150,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; feature-length or series immersive non-fiction works

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

The BFI Doc Society Expanded Screen Fund supports ambitious screen-based works of non-fiction intended to reach audiences beyond cinemas, including VR, AR, MR, and 360-degree experiences. Projects must be grounded in real events, locations, or narratives and must demonstrate a clear creative rationale for their technical approach. AMOUNT: £25,000–£150,000 non-recoupable grant. ELIGIBILITY: producers with a demonstrated track record in delivering immersive work; UK-based; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk-taking, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. DEADLINE: 22 June 2026 at 1pm UK time. APPLY via the BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Summerhall Arts Studio: Live Performance Development Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Summerhall Arts

Location: Edinburgh, Scotland, UK

Award: Up to GBP 3,100 project budget (agreed between the Lead Artist and Summerhall Arts), plus five days in a dedicated space, technical support and access to simple lighting/sound/AV, creative/producing/technical guidance, and an informal end-of-week sharing. No application fee.

Duration: Five days, 12-16 October 2026 (10am-5pm)

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

Summerhall Arts Studios are dedicated residencies for the early development of new theatre and cross-disciplinary live performance work, giving artists and collaborators time and space to test ideas, explore approaches, and begin shaping work intended for live audiences. They are about experimentation, exploration and play rather than finished scripts or fully realised productions, and are especially suited to projects that benefit from working in real space: testing form, staging, sound, movement, liveness, audience relationship or collaborative process. Applications are welcome from artists working across theatre, movement, performance, interdisciplinary practice and other hybrid live-performance forms. INCLUDES: up to GBP 3,100 project budget; five days in a dedicated space (Tech Cube Zero, or the Anatomy Lecture Theatre if more suitable); technical support and access to simple lighting, sound and AV; advice from the Summerhall Arts creative, producing and technical team; and an informal end-of-week sharing with peers and/or invited industry colleagues. WHAT THEY SEEK: early-stage work that benefits from practical, collaborative or technical exploration, intended for live audiences, with clear and achievable plans for the five days; they are less likely to support purely desk-based script development or projects already substantially resourced by other major development funding. Apply via the online form by Sunday 21 June 2026 (11pm); outcomes notified by 3 July; studio dates 12-16 October 2026. Access requirements or alternative application formats: imogen@summerhallarts.co.uk.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ESRC Digital Good Network: Artwork Commission (Expressions of Interest)
item.description:
Organisation: ESRC Digital Good Network

Location: UK (artists based outside the UK welcome; work can be done remotely)

Award: Up to GBP 40,000 for the final commission, inclusive of VAT (capped at GBP 33,333 for artists based outside the UK due to reverse-charge VAT). Up to five shortlisted teams each receive GBP 1,000 to develop a proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Commission runs October 2026 to February 2027; first exhibition June 2027

Deadline: Closed 01 Jul 2026

The ESRC Digital Good Network is commissioning an artwork or visualisation that represents its research on the question 'what does a good digital society look like, and how do we get there?' The final piece must be simple, visually engaging and effective for multiple audiences, and will be shown in physical exhibitions (first in June 2027), online (interactive elements possible), and on a Z-fold leaflet. Two-stage process: submit an Expression of Interest (a portfolio link plus an optional one-page PDF on your approach) by 1 July 2026, 4pm UK time, to applications@digitalgood.net; up to five teams are shortlisted and paid GBP 1,000 each to develop full proposals (Aug-Sep 2026); one is commissioned in October 2026 to make the final work iteratively with two Network team members (Nov 2026-Feb 2027). Artists may visualise the Network's funded projects, its 'building blocks' of a good digital society, the values behind the Digital Good Index, or any aspect of the work. Reference points include Kate Crawford's Anatomy of AI and Calculating Empires, Dear Data by Stefanie Posavec and Giorgia Lupi, and the OECD Better Life Index. ELIGIBILITY: artists or visualisers worldwide, working alone or in teams; an optional online Q&A is held 10 June 2026. NOTE: the final output is expected NOT to use AI; if AI is used in the process, applicants must disclose how. 2D artworks have panel production and transport covered; 3D artworks must cover their own production, transport and installation.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Visual Arts Scotland x Bothy Project: Sweeney's Bothy Residency 2026 (Isle of Eigg)
item.description:
Organisation: Visual Arts Scotland (VAS), in partnership with Bothy Project

Location: Sweeney's Bothy, Isle of Eigg, Scottish Inner Hebrides, UK (off-grid)

Award: GBP 720 artist fee, plus reasonable travel up to GBP 250 and accommodation costs included. No application fee.

Duration: One-week residency (10 to 17 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Sixth year of Visual Arts Scotland's residency in partnership with Bothy Project, offering a week at Sweeney's Bothy, an off-grid, purpose-built artist residency space on the Isle of Eigg in the Scottish Inner Hebrides. As the residency is only a week long, there is no expectation to produce a body of work; the purpose is time to reflect on practice, develop ideas and engage with the island's environment and culture. ELIGIBILITY: open to any creative discipline (visual arts, craft and design, music, literature, performance, and researchers/thinkers), but applicants must be a VAS member at both the time of application and the time of residency. Applicants are asked to be mindful of the island community's ethos of environmental sustainability and to travel sustainably (cars are not permitted for non-residents). The successful applicant undertakes a VAS Instagram takeover and documents their experience. Apply by email to admin@visualartsscotland.org plus the online form, with six images of recent work on a single PDF.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency 2026: Artists Based in Greece
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks (London)

Location: Gasworks, London, United Kingdom

Award: Fully funded 11-week residency: 24/7 private studio at Gasworks, single-room accommodation in Gasworks Residency House, economy return flights + taxi transfers from home country, all visa costs and visa-application support, weekly stipend of GBP 175 for living expenses, materials budget up to GBP 800, prepaid TFL Zones 1+2 travel card for the residency, studio visits with staff/curators/artists, an Open Studio event and presentation, dedicated residency page on the Gasworks website with a short video interview, networking and at least two individual studio visits, plus admin/pastoral/curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 11 weeks: 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Gasworks residency open call for a contemporary visual artist at a pivotal point in their career, based in Greece. The 11-week, fully funded residency takes place at Gasworks in London 30 September - 15 December 2026. The programme supports professional development, cultural exchange and artistic experimentation; up to 20 residencies per year, self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based. Selection panel includes Gasworks reps + external advisors with specialist knowledge of the Greek and UK contemporary art scenes; a shortlist of four artists is invited to a short online interview before the final decision. ELIGIBILITY: contemporary visual artists (not duos/collectives) based in Greece, with at least a moderate level of spoken English; artists studying outside Greece may apply if they have a permanent address in Greece and will be living there during the residency dates. Priority is given to artists who have not previously worked or studied in London. APPLY: one PDF (max 15MB) with cover sheet, 250-word practice statement, 250-word residency-plan statement, up to 15 images or video/sound clips of recent work (with captions), CV (max 3 pages), and any relevant documentation; spoken responses can substitute the written statements via embedded video/audio links. Deadline 1 June 2026, 1pm UK time. Supported by Thalia and Alex Haidas.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency 2026: Artists Based in Aotearoa New Zealand
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks (London), with the Office for Contemporary Art Aotearoa

Location: Gasworks, London, United Kingdom

Award: Fully funded 11-week residency: 24/7 private studio at Gasworks, single-room accommodation in Gasworks Residency House, economy return flights + taxi transfers from home country (1 cabin + 1 hold bag), all visa costs and visa-application support, weekly stipend of GBP 175, materials budget up to GBP 800, prepaid TFL Zones 1+2 travel card, studio visits, an Open Studio event on Saturday 5 December 2026, a dedicated residency page on the Gasworks website with short video interview, networking + at least two individual studio visits, plus admin/pastoral/curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 11 weeks: 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Gasworks residency open call for an early-career contemporary visual artist based in Aotearoa New Zealand. The 11-week, fully funded residency takes place at Gasworks in London 30 September - 15 December 2026. Self-led, non-prescriptive, process-based programme supporting professional development, cultural exchange and experimentation. Selection by panel of Gasworks staff and external advisors with specialist knowledge of the Aotearoa NZ and UK contemporary-art scenes; priority for artists who have not yet worked in London; shortlist of four invited to a short online interview (week beginning 22 June 2026); successful applicant notified the week beginning 6 July 2026. ELIGIBILITY: early-career contemporary visual artists based in Aotearoa NZ only; at least moderate spoken English; no duos/collectives, no accompanying partners/children supported. APPLY: one PDF (max 15MB) with cover sheet, 250-word practice statement, 250-word residency-plan statement, up to 15 images/video/sound clips of recent work (captions, up to 100 words per work), CV (max 3 pages), and any relevant documentation; spoken responses via embedded video/audio links accepted. Deadline 1 June 2026, 1pm UK time. Supported by the Jan Warburton Charitable Trust and Aotearoa NZ Friends of Gasworks, with additional support from the British Council Aotearoa NZ and the Pacific, in partnership with the Office for Contemporary Art Aotearoa.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency 2027: Artists Based in the Caribbean
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks (London), supported by Suzanne McFayden + The Nelumbo Collection

Location: Gasworks, London, United Kingdom

Award: Fully funded 11-week residency: 24/7 private studio at Gasworks, single-room accommodation in Gasworks Residency House, economy return flights + taxi transfers from home country (1 cabin + 1 hold bag), all visa costs and visa-application support, weekly stipend of GBP 175, materials budget up to GBP 800, prepaid TFL Zones 1+2 travel card, studio visits, Open Studio event on Saturday 13 March 2027, dedicated residency page on the Gasworks website with short video interview, networking + at least two individual studio visits, plus admin/pastoral/curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 11 weeks: 6 January - 24 March 2027

Deadline: Closed 29 Jun 2026

Gasworks residency open call for an early-career contemporary visual artist based in the Caribbean. The 11-week, fully funded residency takes place at Gasworks in London 6 January - 24 March 2027. Self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based programme supporting professional development, cultural exchange and experimentation. Selection by panel of Gasworks reps + external advisors with specialist knowledge of the Caribbean and UK contemporary-art scenes; priority for artists who have not previously worked in London; shortlist of four invited to a short online interview before the final decision. ELIGIBLE COUNTRIES/TERRITORIES (per the UN Geoscheme for the Caribbean): Anguilla, Antigua and Barbuda, Aruba, Bahamas, Barbados, Bonaire/Sint Eustatius and Saba, British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Cuba, Curacao, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Grenada, Guadeloupe, Haiti, Jamaica, Martinique, Montserrat, Puerto Rico, Saint Barthelemy, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Sint Maarten, Trinidad and Tobago, Turks and Caicos Islands, United States Virgin Islands. Applicants must have at least moderate spoken English; no duos/collectives, partners or children supported on-site. APPLY: one PDF (max 15MB) with cover sheet, 250-word practice statement, 250-word residency-plan statement, up to 15 images/video/sound clips of recent work (captions, up to 100 words per work), CV (max 3 pages), and any relevant documentation; spoken responses via embedded video/audio links accepted. Deadline 29 June 2026, 1pm UK time. Supported by Suzanne McFayden + The Nelumbo Collection.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artists' Futures Fund: Breakthrough Artist Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Artists' Futures Fund

Location: England and Wales, UK (in-kind studio space at a partner institution)

Award: GBP 10,000 unrestricted bursary over the 10-month fellowship, plus in-kind studio space, mentorship from a partner institution, professional development, and a co-curated end-of-fellowship group showcase. No application fee.

Duration: 10 months (October 2026 to July 2027)

Deadline: Closed 12 Jun 2026

Needs-based fellowship from the Artists' Futures Fund bridging the gap from graduation to professional visual-arts practice. ELIGIBILITY: recent BA or MA visual-arts graduates (within two years of October 2026) from an AFF partner institution (Cardiff School of Art & Design, Chichester College Group, Liverpool Hope, Loughborough, Manchester Metropolitan, City of Portsmouth College, Sunderland, Swansea College of Art UWTSD), or a relevant Level 3 UAL Diploma/A Levels from City of Portsmouth College; must be eligible to live and work in England or Wales, facing socio-economic, mental and/or physical health barriers, and able to commit fully to the 10 months without additional formal study. Applications open 15 May 2026. Apply via the Artists' Futures Fund support page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arts Council England: Developing Your Creative Practice (DYCP) Round 24
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council England

Location: England, UK

Award: GBP 1,000 to GBP 12,000 per individual for creative-practice development (research, training, time to think, travel, mentoring, etc.). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (practice-development period)

Deadline: Closed 02 Jul 2026

Developing Your Creative Practice (DYCP) supports individual creative and cultural practitioners in England to take time to develop their practice, across all disciplines including digital and media arts. The funding is for practice development (research, training, mentoring, travel, experimentation) rather than producing or presenting finished work. ELIGIBILITY: England-based independent creative practitioners with a track record who work outside the major-funded organisations. Round 24 opens 4 June 2026 (12:00) and closes 2 July 2026. Apply via the Arts Council England DYCP page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Library: Creative Fellowship 2026 - Remote Ecologies (Art, Climate and the Amazon)
item.description:
Organisation: The British Library

Location: London / UK-based

Award: GBP 7,500 stipend plus up to GBP 3,000 production budget.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027

Deadline: Closed 02 Jun 2026

A collection-based artistic research fellowship at the British Library responding to climate and the Amazon through the Library's holdings. The fellow develops new work informed by the collections over roughly a year (September 2026 to July 2027), receiving a GBP 7,500 stipend and up to GBP 3,000 for production. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based artists, over 18, not currently a student, with a public track record and an interest in collection-based research and climate issues.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Hugo Burge Foundation: Grants for Creative Individuals 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Hugo Burge Foundation

Location: United Kingdom

Award: Up to GBP 5,000 per individual (can fund up to 100% of a project under GBP 10,000). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Project grants from the Hugo Burge Foundation supporting individual creatives across all disciplines based in the UK, with a focus on craftsmanship, creative communities and education. The Creative Individuals stream awards up to GBP 5,000 and can cover up to 100% of a project budget under GBP 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: practitioners in all creative disciplines based across the UK. Applications open 1 June 2026 via Submittable and close 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: John Hansard Gallery: Open Call for Disabled Artists 2026 (Future Curators)
item.description:
Organisation: John Hansard Gallery, with the University of Southampton (part of the DASH Future Curators Programme)

Location: Within 40 miles of John Hansard Gallery, Southampton, UK

Award: GBP 2,000 artist fee (including up to four meetings with students), plus travel expenses, access costs and a materials budget to be defined. No application fee.

Duration: Project running July 2026 to a February 2027 exhibition

Deadline: Closed 25 May 2026

Open call from John Hansard Gallery for one disabled artist to collaborate with Occupational Therapy and Engineering students at the University of Southampton to make or adapt tools that support their artistic practice. The tools and resulting artwork are displayed in an exhibition at John Hansard Gallery in February 2027, curated by Jess Starns as part of the Future Curators Programme, a disability-justice residency programme for Disabled curators founded by the Disabled artist-led organisation DASH. The work involves collaborating with students in July and August, testing and refining the tools through autumn, and creating artwork for the exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: disabled artists living and working within a 40-mile distance of John Hansard Gallery. Submit an Expression of Interest via the gallery's open-call page; access requirements supported (contact jennifa@jhg.art).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency Open Call 2026 (Artists based in Southern Africa)
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks

Location: London, United Kingdom (residency held at Gasworks; applicants must be based in Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa or Zimbabwe)

Award: Fully funded residency: single-room accommodation in Gasworks' Residency House (shared two-storey house with three communal bathrooms and a kitchen); economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175 for living expenses; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card for unlimited central London travel throughout the residency.

Duration: Eleven weeks, 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 18 May 2026

Eleven-week fully funded residency at Gasworks in London for an early-career artist based in Southern Africa (Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa, Zimbabwe). Gasworks' residency programme supports professional development, cultural exchange, and artistic experimentation, hosting up to twenty residencies a year. Residencies are self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based: artists are expected to be self-motivated and to lead on the research and production of their own work, while Gasworks provides administrative, pastoral and curatorial support. Includes 24/7 access to a fully accessible private studio at Gasworks alongside resident artists and a community of London-based studio holders. ELIGIBILITY: early-career artist based in Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa, or Zimbabwe.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Serpentine: Future Art Ecosystems R&D Fellowship - Art x Convergence (2026-2027)
item.description:
Organisation: Serpentine Arts Technologies (Future Art Ecosystems)

Location: London, United Kingdom (low-residency hybrid; three in-person weekend intensives in London required)

Award: £10,000 award per fellow (4 fellows: individuals or collectives), plus travel and accommodation support for the three London intensives, plus specialist seminars, workshops and structured peer learning through the cohort.

Duration: Six months, September 2026 to March 2027 (low-residency hybrid). Biweekly online cohort sessions and bespoke mentorship plus three in-person weekend intensives in London: 16-20 September 2026, 11-15 November 2026, and 24-28 March 2027.

Deadline: Closed 07 Jun 2026

Inaugural Future Art Ecosystems (FAE) R&D Fellowship from Serpentine Arts Technologies, supporting individual practitioners and ecosystem development in art and advanced technologies. Four fellows (individuals or collectives) selected. Open to artists, curators, technologists, organisers and producers working at the intersection of art and advanced technologies. International applicants welcome provided they can attend the three in-person London intensives. Inaugural theme: Art x Convergence, framed as a prompt to explore how AI is reshaping cultural and societal systems and to develop new frameworks for embodiment, robotics, legal constructs, markets and planetary organisation in light of AI's capacity to pursue goals, model environments and act in the world. Fellows pursue a defined research question connected to their practice or a project in development, supported through professional and specialist mentorship, network development, cohort exchange, and public-facing process sharing. FAE was initiated by Victoria Ivanova in 2019; programme led by Tommie Introna, Tsige Tafesse, and Kay Watson. Info sessions online on 15 May and 22 May 2026. Application deadline midnight BST 7 June 2026. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Cavendish Arts Science Fellowship 2026-2027 (Girton College, Cambridge)
item.description:
Organisation: Cavendish Arts Science (Cavendish Laboratory) in partnership with Girton College, University of Cambridge

Location: Cambridge, United Kingdom (residency in Cambridge for at least 6 months up to 1 year)

Award: Stipend £10,000 (inclusive of taxes) plus rent-free accommodation and meals at Girton College during the residency, £10,000 production budget for new work, and up to £3,000 travel budget. Total cash and in-kind value approximately £23,000 plus accommodation and meals.

Duration: One year (October 2026 to October 2027); residency in Cambridge of at least 6 months up to 1 year, with exact dates flexible.

Deadline: Closed 16 May 2026

Annual fellowship for one artist to develop new work-in-progress through engagement with physicists at the Cavendish Laboratory and researchers in adjacent fields. Open internationally; not confined to any single aesthetic, theme, or medium. Artists with no previous experience working with scientists or in scientific environments are explicitly encouraged to apply. The brief favours adventurous artists exploring alternative ways of knowing the world and working with communities not privileged in mainstream science. Past Fellows include Logan Dandridge, Ain Bailey, Robert Ssempijja, Akeelah Bertram, and Thulani Rachia, with practices spanning experimental film, sound, dance, and immersive technology. Strong fit for critical-AI / data / surveillance / sensory-translation practices framed as artistic-scientific dialogue. Application by online form only. Deadline 16 May 2026 at 11am BST. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Peckham Digital: Programme Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Peckham Digital

Location: Peckham, London, United Kingdom (in-person festival 22 to 25 October 2026)

Award: Paid: facilitators and speakers compensated at Artist Union England pay rates (contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding application; if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead)

Duration: Festival programme slot (22 to 25 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Open call for the 5th edition of Peckham Digital, a festival celebrating creative computing. This call is for the PROGRAMME track: artists, creative technologists and creative coders to facilitate workshops, present talks, provide demos, or deliver performances (the separate Artwork Open Call covers exhibition pieces). Emerging applicants explicitly welcomed; over half of past Peckham Digital artists had this as their first paid professional exhibition. Selected facilitators/speakers paid at Artist Union England rates. Workshop facilitators are asked whether their software will be open-source. Application requires: type of contribution (demo/workshop/talk/performance/other), 200-word description (text or video), 250-word facilitator statement on experience, 200 words on professional development impact, sample images or video, technical requirements, and any access needs. Equal Opportunities form also requested. Important caveat: festival is contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding being confirmed; if their funding application is not successful the festival will not go ahead.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Peckham Digital: Artwork Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Peckham Digital

Location: Peckham, London, United Kingdom (in-person festival 22 to 25 October 2026)

Award: Paid: each selected artist receives a fee (contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding application; outcome expected early July 2026; if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead). Submission to the open call is free.

Duration: Festival exhibition slot (22 to 25 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Open call for the 5th edition of Peckham Digital, a festival celebrating creative computing in all its shapes and forms. This call is for the ARTWORK EXHIBITION track only (the separate Programme Open Call covers talks, workshops, demos, performances and films). Looking for artists, creative technologists, creative coders and performers to exhibit artworks. Emerging applicants and early-career creative technologists explicitly welcomed; over half of past Peckham Digital artists had this as their first paid professional exhibition. Submission to the open call is free; selected artists receive a fee. Important caveat: festival is contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding being confirmed (decision expected early July 2026); if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead. Read the Application Guidelines before submitting.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: William Morris Art Futures Bursary
item.description:
Organisation: House of Hackney, William Morris Gallery & IntoUniversity

Location: London Boroughs of Hackney and Waltham Forest, UK

Award: Up to £10,000

Duration: One-off bursary

Deadline: Closed 11 May 2026

New bursary from House of Hackney and William Morris Gallery, in partnership with IntoUniversity, supporting the next generation of artists in the London Boroughs of Hackney and Waltham Forest. Provides up to £10,000 to 16 to 25 year olds with creative potential who face barriers to accessing education, training, materials or mentorship. Inspired by William Morris's values of art for all and the transformative power of creativity.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: XAIxArts 4: 4th International Workshop on Explainable AI for the Arts
item.description:
Organisation: ACM Creativity & Cognition (University of the Arts London)

Location: London, UK

Award: Workshop participation (funding TBC)

Duration: 1 day workshop, 13 July 2026

Deadline: Closed 23 Apr 2026

Call for papers, pictorials or videos for a workshop bringing together HCI, interaction design, AI, explainable AI and digital-arts researchers and practitioners to examine explainability through creative and artistic domains. Notifications 14 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
